title: Brian Li
text:
Hello People!
This is my E-Portfolio landing page. My name is Brian Li, a current MGEM student at UBC. I was born and raised in China, and studied biology and urban forestry for my bachelor’s degree. I found geo-spatial analysis is pretty fun and we enjoy the time playing with data. Here are links to my professional social media.
Contact me if you have the same interest or looking for any help.

title: Resume
text:
Education
The University of British Columbia (UBC) | Vancouver, BC, Canada 09/2025–05/2026
Master of Geomatics for Environmental Management
The University of British Columbia (UBC) | Vancouver, BC, Canada 09/2021–08/2024
Bachelor of Science in Urban Forestry with Honours, Minor in Urban Greenspace Management
Dean of Forestry Scholarship (2023–2024)
Fujian Agriculture and Forestry University (FAFU) | Fujian, China 09/2018–08/2021
Bachelor of Science in Ecology


Academic Experience
Urban Heat Island Effect Visualization Project, UBC, Vancouver, BC 09/2022–04/2023

Generated the Heat Vulnerable Areas and Ocean Heat Capacity maps by using ArcGIS and ENVI remote sensing
Analyzed the Urban Heat Island Effect by incorporating the GIS database of land surface temperature, vegetation classification, building, roads, and campus boundary from satellite images
Accurately conducted a regression analysis of the geodata, discovering correlation between parameters regarding the p-value and R square value in Office Excel

Water-Crisis Research Project, UBC, Vancouver, BC 11/2022–01/2023

Conducted thorough research on water conservation policies and initiatives in South Africa to discover potential implications from the case study of the Day Zero in Cape Town City
Facilitated ongoing discussions with team members and mentor through weekly online meetings, forming meeting records, detailed bibliography and the research question
Composed a well-arranged poster with diagrams and references to interpret the water crisis in Cape Town City and proposed a couple of potential solutions, integrating the government, organizations, and citizens’ inner power

Forest Therapy Project Assistant, UBC, Vancouver, BC 06/2023–09/2023

Independently associated the curriculum development for the ‘International Forestry’ course of ten comprehensive chapters, summarizing concepts and case studies from primary academic references while ensuring meticulous alignment of content
Conducted intensive literature reviews, website and relevant multimedia resources searching for each lecture chapter, while appropriately citing all references and producing 100 pages of slides per week

Urban Forestry Research Assistant, UBC, Vancouver, BC 06/2022–09/2022

Collected and classified documents on urban forestry from all Canadian municipalities to create a comprehensive database to help with the decision-making of researchers and governments
Skimmed through government documents including stormwater management, climate change mitigation, wildfire strategy, street tree bylaw, invasive species control strategy, and land use plan. Incorporated all relevant documents and contributed to database build-up



Work & Volunteer
Land Surveyor Assistant, Amray Land Surveying Ltd., Burnaby, BC 10/2024–01/2025

Efficiently and precisely set up Total Station at various locations, controlling the errors below 2 centimetres or 5 millimetres depending on the projects, and recording detailed field notes
Familiar with reading surveying maps and the process of measuring different kinds of projects, including posting, reposting, on-site layout, topography, new engineering contract, and benchmark setting
Precisely recorded the corresponding code of measured objects, exporting the data to the database for designers

Beaty Biodiversity Museum Educator, UBC, Vancouver, BC 10/2023–05/2024

Provided engaging interpretations of creature and fossil specimens to museum visitors with inclusive language and customer service attitude, informing instructions of touchable specimens to visitors

Answered and addressed questions about specimens, museum layout, and seasonal activities for visitors, communicating with fellow educators via radio and collaborating to facilitate smooth closing of the museum

Class Notetaker, UBC, Vancouver, BC 10/2022–01/2024

Recorded real-time, detailed notes during professor’s lectures for students with disabilities and uploaded notes to the website twice a week with well-organized information
Consistently attended all classes punctually, maintaining open communication with the professor to ensure accuracy and completeness of notes

Inclusive Forestry Peer Mentor, UBC, Vancouver, BC 05/2023–08/2023

Shared valuable insights and experiences of living in Canada and studying at UBC with program newcomers, fostering a sense of learning community and support
Actively participated in a peer mentor seminar discussing issues such as academic plagiarism, contributing personal experiences to enrich and facilitate workshops
Provided insightful advice to event organizers on crucial topics including traffic regulations, migration document extensions, and other related matters, ensuring smooth event operations

Eco-Steward, Stanley Park, Vancouver, BC 08/2022–04/2023

Identified and removed patches of invasive species (e.g., Himalayan blackberry, English ivy)
Planted native species (e.g., vine maple, huckleberry, deer fern, etc.) in Stanley Park by finding a suitable position to reach a higher survival rate
Watered new-planted vegetation in the following weeks and approximately 80% of them survived the drought



Qualifications

Software: Proficient in ArcGIS Pro, QGIS, Google Earth Engine, Rstudio, and Microsoft Office, and capable with Python coding, Adobe Creative Cloud Suits, i-Tree tool kit, Rhino-3D modelling, and ENVI remote sensing tools
Research ability: Demonstrated capability of conducting comprehensive literature reviews independently, efficiently gathering and analyzing information from diverse sources
Academic Writing & Communication: Demonstrated ability to produce high-quality essays and reports. Strong capability in delivering presentations to diverse audiences in both written reports and verbal communication
Bilingual Speaker: English (Proficient), Mandarin (Native)

title: Publications
text:
Peer-reviewed publications:
Despite listing them on your resume, it may also be pertinent to create a seperate tab for publications and reports. As your career progresses, this list may become quite long, so be sure to organize things. You may want to point to ‘most recent’ publications, or categorize things by project/topic.
